response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

During the promotional week, the customer will pay 15% less on each item. The handcrafted table originally priced at $200 will cost $200 - (15% of $200) = $200 - $30 = $170. The set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150 will cost $150 - (15% of $150) = $150 - $22.50 = $127.50. The total amount the customer will pay for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. Reach for the discount, and the customer will save $30 on their purchase. ***

Sarah's shop is a great place for customers to buy handmade products and support local factory workers. The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items, which means customers can enjoy a better price on their purchases. The handcrafted table and the set of factory-made chairs are both eligible for the discount. The customer will pay $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. ***

The customer will pay $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. The discount applies to each item separately, so the handcrafted table is reduced by 15% and the set of factory-made chairs is also reduced by 15%. Reach for the discount, and the customer will save $30 on their purchase. The word "yard" is used to describe the size of the table, and the word "milk" is used to describe the type of product being sold. ***
